rules: --- SALES TABLE ROUTING ---
For general retail sales (non-China, non-ecom) use ads.ads_ckg_sales_di.
For ecommerce / online sales (non-China) use ads.ads_ckg_ecom_salesfact.
For China retail / offline sales use ads.ads_ckc_retail_business_sales_di.
For China online / ecommerce sales use ads.ads_ckc_online_business_sales_di.
For SF2 (Salesforce2) sales use ads.ads_ecom_sf2_sales_hi.
--- STOCK TABLE ROUTING ---
For stock / SOH / inventory (non-China, non-ecom) use ads.ads_ckg_stock_wi.
For China retail stock / SOH use ads.ads_ckc_stock_wi.
For China ecommerce stock / SOH use ads.ads_ckc_ecom_stock_di.
For stock type analysis at country+article level use ads.ads_ckg_stock_type_wi; join to sales/stock tables on country, year, week, article.
For stock type analysis at country+article+colour level use ads.ads_ckg_sku_stock_type_di; join to sales/stock tables on country, year, week, article, colour.
--- STOCK DATE ROUNDING (non-China) ---

Stock data in ads.ads_ckg_stock_wi is captured weekly on Saturday. When filtering by soh_dt, use >= (not =) with the nearest Saturday date, because different countries report SOH on Saturday, Sunday, or Monday. Use: soh_dt >=  (requested_date::date - ((EXTRACT(DOW FROM requested_date::date)::int + 1) % 7) * INTERVAL '1 day')::date. Example: 2026-05-28 (Thursday) → 2026-05-23 (Saturday). Saturday stays the same, Sunday goes back 1 day, Monday goes back 2 days, etc. Apply this rounding to ALL non-China stock queries regardless of which day of week the user specifies.
--- TRAFFIC TABLE ROUTING ---
For retail traffic (non-China) use ads.ads_ckg_traffic_di.
For China retail traffic use ads.ads_ckc_traffic_di.
For China ecommerce traffic use ads.ads_ckc_ecom_traffic_di.
For SF2 / digital traffic use ads.ads_ga4_bi_traffic_by_device.
--- DIMENSION TABLES ---
For article / item / product information (description, brand, category) use dim.item_master.
For shop / store details (shop number, store name, country, store category) use dim.shop_master.
--- OTHER ---
For sell-through rate data use ads.business_ckg_sell_through_v1_di.
--- GENERAL RULES ---
Dimension text values (country, brand, store_category etc.) are stored UPPERCASE in the database.
For sold quantity / units use SUM(sales_qty).
For stock / inventory quantity use SUM(soh).
Use sales_dt = 'YYYY-MM-DD' for single day filter.
Use soh_dt >= rounded_saturday_date (never soh_dt =) for stock filters — different countries report on Sat/Sun/Mon so >= captures all reporters in that week.
Always use explicit schema prefix in table references e.g. ads.ads_ckg_sales_di not just ads_ckg_sales_di.
